action.reasoning: Observation: The current best model (ConvNeXt Tiny, 30 epochs) achieves 72.67% accuracy but severely overfits to noisy labels (train_acc ~0.88), causing cleanlab to find 0 label issues because it confidently predicts the noisy labels. Analysis: To make label cleaning work, we need a noise-detector model that has NOT memorized the noise. Training only the classification head on top of a frozen pretrained backbone for a few epochs will learn the dominant signal without overfitting to all 40% noise. Decision: 1) Rewrite train.py with robust freeze_backbone support. 2) Train a 5-epoch frozen-head noise detector with no MixUp/label smoothing so cleanlab gets sharp, meaningful probabilities. 3) Run relabel.py using that checkpoint. 4) In the next iteration, train a full model on the cleaned labels.
action.command: python train.py --model convnext_tiny --epochs 5 --freeze_backbone_epochs 5 --batch_size 256 --lr 1e-2 --weight_decay 0.01 --label_smoothing 0.0 --mixup_alpha 0.0 --warmup_epochs 0 --checkpoint_dir checkpoints/noise_detector --save_prefix nd_ && python relabel.py --checkpoint checkpoints/noise_detector/nd_best_convnext_tiny.pt --output train_cleaned.pt
